name: Llama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70B
license: Proprietary — enterprise/partner access only (no public license terms published; not an open-weight release)
archDisplay: 70B dense transformer (Llama-3.1 architecture, Japanese continual pretrain + instruction tuning)
note: Latest ELYZA LLM for JP flagship, built on Llama 3.1 for longer context and improved Japanese instruction-following vs. the earlier Llama-3-based 70B; distributed only via demo and enterprise API/joint-development deals, not as public open weights.
verificationNotes:
Real, confirmed release: ELYZA's own news post and PR Times release (both 2024-10-25), plus corroborating JP tech press (Nikkei xTECH, Mynavi, CodeZine), confirm a 70B (700-oku-parameter) Llama-3.1-based Japanese model with improved long-context/instruction-following over the prior Llama-3 70B. CORRECTED openness from 'open-weights' to 'closed': ELYZA's press release explicitly states the model is 'provided to companies in various forms through secure API services and joint development projects' (enterprise-only), not released as public weights — only a hosted demo is public. Fetching https://huggingface.co/elyza/Llama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70B returns HTTP 401 'Invalid username or password' (gated/private repo), unlike ELYZA's genuinely open 8B sibling models on HF. License field corrected accordingly since no public license text exists for this restricted-access model. ctxK=128 and langCount=2 are reasonable carry-overs from the Llama-3.1 base architecture but are not explicitly confirmed by ELYZA for this JP variant specifically. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No citable benchmark results found for Llama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70B (corpus/models/elyza/llama-3-1-elyza-jp-70b.json) after a widened, Japanese-language-first search. Checked: (1) ELYZA's own sources — elyza.ai official news post (2024-10-25), the PR Times release (prtimes.jp/main/html/rd/p/000000052.000047565.html, already in specSources), and note.com/elyza + zenn.dev/elyza blog archives — all state only qualitative claims ("exceeds GPT-4/Claude 3 Sonnet/Gemini 1.5 Flash on ELYZA Tasks 100 and Japanese MT-Bench") with no numeric scores in the fetched text for this specific model. (2) Japanese tech press: Nikkei xTECH/Active, IT Leaders (it.impress.co.jp), Mynavi (news.mynavi.jp), CodeZine, ASCII.jp — same qualitative-only coverage. (3) Japanese leaderboards: Nejumi LLM Leaderboard 3/4 (wandb.ai, github.com/wandb/llm-leaderboard) and Swallow LLM Leaderboard (swallow-llm.github.io) — neither leaderboard lists this model by name, consistent with it being gated/closed (the existing verificationNotes already confirms HF repo returns HTTP 401). (4) International sources: Artificial Analysis, OpenRouter, LMArena/Chatbot Arena — no listings found. Important finding to flag: exact numeric scores DO exist publicly (Japanese MT-Bench 9.075, ELYZA Tasks 100 4.070, both beating GPT-4's 9.013/4.030) but they belong to the EARLIER, distinctly-named sibling model 'Llama-3-ELYZA-JP-70B' (released 2024-06-26, Llama-3-based), not 'Llama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70B' (released 2024-10-25, Llama-3.1-based, the model in this corpus file) — multiple secondary sources conflate the two in AI-generated summaries, so this must not be misattributed. Verdict: this remains a real case of no public benchmark data existing for a specific closed/enterprise-only model release; recommend concluding the search rather than running further rounds. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Llama-3.1-ELYZA-JP-70B (corpus/models/elyza/llama-3-1-elyza-jp-70b.json): checked the two specific structured sources requested, no matches found in either.

(1) Epoch AI raw benchmark data: fetched both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB — this file turned out to be benchmark *metadata*, i.e. descriptions of each of the ~76 benchmarks Epoch tracks, not per-model scores) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, 5981 rows — this is the actual per-model-run scores feed, with columns for model name, HF developer id, org, benchmark, score, etc.). Grepped both files case-insensitively for "elyza" (0 hits in either file) and for "swallow" (0 hits) and for any "llama-3.1...jp..." pattern (0 hits). Epoch AI simply does not track this model under any name.

(2) Vals.ai: fetched https://www.vals.ai/models listing page (198KB HTML) — no "elyza"/"llama-3.1"/"swallow" string present... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]
